title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: The war premium slammed back into crude. Brent jumped about 5% to $78 and WTI to about $73.5 after three tankers were struck in the Strait of Hormuz, US forces hit more than 80 Iranian targets, and the president called the accord 'over.' A week ago the barrel was in glut; the same chokepoint re-armed it in a single session.

The June Fed minutes killed the cut. Inflation risks tilted up, a few participants wanted an immediate hike, and futures moved September-hike odds to 65.7%. The oil shock only sharpens the bias, and the tape now reads shocks as inflationary rather than growth-scaring.

Gold fell even as the war flared — spot bullion off about 0.9% to $4,067 — because higher yields and a firmer dollar overrode the safe-haven bid. The tell is that the market fears the Fed more than the strait right now.

props.caption: The chokepoint that let the war premium bleed out re-armed it in a single session

items.why: Trump said any resumed fighting would go fast; watch whether the tanker war widens or the strikes deter, with the strait at SEVERE and oil trading it tick by tick.
The desk owned this down to near zero after the $78 spike; only a sudden, credible de-escalation gets crude back under $70 before the deadline.
USD/JPY closed at 162.46, one tick from the line; the call flipped toward YES as the dollar rode the hawkish minutes and the oil-driven yield move.
The desk opened this at p=0.58; the minutes and the oil shock argue for a hike, but two months of data and any growth crack could stay the Fed's hand.

items.what: World Cup · the quarter-finals in the heat
items.why: The last eight play through the heat window, with the Atlanta 15:00 semi on 15 July the arc's next live test of scheduling versus the bodies on the grass.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-08 · Foreman, Graves, Tinkerton, Cogsworth · CAS call settled today (miss)

open_calls.question: The FOMC raises its policy rate at the September meeting
open_calls.detail: The June minutes showed inflation risks tilted up and a few members ready to hike now; futures put September odds at 65.7%, and the oil spike sharpens the inflationary read. The desk sits just under the market: two months of data and any growth crack could still stay the Fed's hand.

open_calls.question: ICE front-month Brent settles below $70.00
open_calls.detail: Owned down hard: Brent spiked about 5% to $78 as the Gulf war reignited, so a sub-$70 settle before the deadline now needs a sudden, credible de-escalation that no party is signalling.

open_calls.question: USD/JPY reaches 162.50 (a retest of its 40-year low)
open_calls.detail: Flipped toward yes: USD/JPY printed 162.46 on the hawkish Fed minutes and the oil-driven yield move, one tick from the line, with the rate gap intact and no disclosed intervention.

open_calls.question: China acknowledges another east-of-Taiwan coast-guard patrol
open_calls.detail: Carried near-certain: Taiwan again logged Chinese military activity around the island and warned of gray-zone normalisation, consistent with the run of east-of-Taiwan patrols.

open_calls.question: A rights-holder publicly discloses break-specific World Cup ad rates before the final
open_calls.detail: Carried: no broadcaster has disclosed break-specific rates; the pause is monetised but its price stays private, and the incentive to publish before the final is weak.

items.what: Brent settled up about 5% near $78 and WTI near $73.5 as the Gulf war reignited, reversing a week in which recovered Hormuz flows had pushed the barrel into glut.
The June FOMC minutes showed broad inflation unease and pricing for a September hike, not a cut; the dollar rose to a multi-week high and USD/JPY hit 162.46.
The chip complex split — a rebound in Nvidia and Broadcom on China H200 headlines and an Apple-Broadcom spending deal followed the prior session's peak-AI-spend washout.
